---
title: "Assignment 1"
subtitle: "Deep Learning for Visual Computing"
author:
  - name: "Ruslan Basyrov"
    email: ruslan.basyrov@tuwien.ac.at
  - name: "Iana Bembeeva"
    email: e52017248@student.tuwien.ac.at
    
bibliography: bibliography.bib
---

## ViT Implementation

The ViT implementation is adapted from [@wangLucidrainsVitpytorch2026] and modified for CIFAR-10 classification.

## Results

### Table 4: Best Configuration per Model

::: {.wideblock}
{{< include build/tables/results_table.md >}}
:::

### Test Accuracy by Model

{{< include build/figures/test_accuracy.qmd >}}

### Figure 1: Validation Mean Per-Class Accuracy

::: {#fig-curves}

{{< include build/figures/val_accuracy_curves.qmd >}}

Validation mean per-class accuracy for the best CNN, ResNet18, and ViT configurations. The checkpoint for each model was selected according to the highest validation mean per-class accuracy.

:::

### Figure 3: CNN Ablation — Augmentation & Regularization

::: {#fig-cnn-ablation}

{{< include build/figures/cnn_ablation.qmd >}}

Validation mean per-class accuracy for selected CNN configurations varying augmentation strategy, weight decay, and dropout.

:::

### Figure 4: ViT Experiments

::: {#fig-vit-ablation}

{{< include build/figures/vit_ablation.qmd >}}

Validation mean per-class accuracy for selected ViT configurations varying augmentation strategy, weight decay, and dropout.

:::
